topic: Canonical-save pipeline invariant research for system-spec-kit

focus: Enumerate the write targets of /memory:save by reading generate-context.ts, workflow.ts around the H-56-1 fix, generate-description.ts, and post-save-review.ts; build the Q1 field catalogue and identify the first candidate cross-layer invariants for Q2.

graphEvents.label: Q1 exhaustive write-field catalogue across frontmatter, description.json, graph-metadata derived fields, and vector index
Q2 cross-layer invariants for canonical save
workflow.ts updates description.json memorySequence, memoryNameHistory, and lastUpdated on every canonical save
H-56-1 lifts planner-mode gating so graph refresh, spec-doc reindex, and post-save review run on every canonical save
memory_save writes validated _memory.continuity frontmatter and aligns routed anchor identity to that surface
createMemoryRecord persists anchor-aware vector index rows plus post-insert metadata for canonical documents
description refresh answers Q1
follow-up parity supports Q2
continuity anchor answers Q1
vector field catalogue answers Q1

focus: Close the Q1 catalogue delta, derive source-backed cross-layer invariants, and verify whether H-56-1 restored metadata side effects for both plan-only and full-auto canonical saves.

graphEvents.label: Q3 H-56-1 scope across full-auto and plan-only dispatch paths
Canonical createMemoryRecord also writes tenant/user/agent/session scope metadata in the post-insert layer when scope is present
description.lastUpdated and graph-metadata derived.last_save_at must match on workflow-driven canonical saves
Workflow-driven graph refreshes must stamp save_lineage same_pass
Frontmatter packet_pointer, description specFolder, and graph spec_folder normalize to the same packet identity
Continuity key_files, vector row filePath plus anchorId, and graph source_docs should point at the same canonical target surface
Plan-only and full-auto canonical saves should produce the same metadata follow-ups after H-56-1
Remaining canonical-save skips are regeneration, env-kill-switch, or research-backfill guards rather than planner-mode asymmetry
scope metadata closes the Q1 delta
same-pass freshness supports Q2
same-pass lineage supports Q2
packet identity supports Q2
target doc parity supports Q2
planner parity supports Q2
remaining gates classify Q3
planner parity answers Q3

focus: Observe Q2 invariants across a representative 026 packet sample, classify divergence types, and decide whether any active packet drops canonical-save state rather than only showing historical metadata skew.

graphEvents.label: description.lastUpdated and graph-metadata derived.last_save_at must match on workflow-driven canonical saves
Workflow-driven graph refreshes must stamp save_lineage same_pass
Frontmatter packet_pointer, description specFolder, and graph spec_folder normalize to the same packet identity
Continuity key_files, vector row filePath plus anchorId, and graph source_docs should point at the same canonical target surface
Plan-only and full-auto canonical saves should produce the same metadata follow-ups after H-56-1
001 research root keeps identity/doc surfaces but shows historical freshness skew and null lineage
004 older in-progress packet keeps identity/doc surfaces but shows old continuity plus null lineage
007 child-only root has metadata files but no spec.md, no continuity, and empty source_docs
010 child-only root has metadata files but no spec.md, no continuity, and empty source_docs
012 shipped packet keeps identity/doc surfaces but description freshness is newer than graph freshness
013 planned packet keeps docs but continuity packet_pointer drops the system-spec-kit prefix
015 fresh in-progress packet has same-pass freshness but description specFolder uses .opencode/specs prefix and lineage is null
019 freshest packet holds same-pass freshness and identity but still has null lineage

focus: Quantify full-tree missing-root-spec scope across 026, determine whether save_lineage is ever persisted, isolate the workflow-to-graph writeback break, and draft validator assertions with migration paths.

graphEvents.label: Full 026 tree scan outside research/review finds four packet roots with description.json and graph-metadata.json but no root spec.md: 007, 008, 009, and 010
All four missing-root-spec packet roots also serialize graph source_docs as an empty array, confirming no canonical doc surface is available to graph metadata
All 96 packet graph-metadata files outside research/review serialize derived.save_lineage as null or missing; none stores a non-null lineage
workflow.ts passes saveLineage same_pass, but mcp_server/api/indexing.ts exports refreshGraphMetadata(specFolder) and drops the options object before graph refresh
Live packet identity drift persists in 013 and 015 across continuity packet_pointer and description.specFolder normalization
012 still shows description.lastUpdated materially newer than graph last_save_at, indicating a partial follow-up asymmetry class remains live
Validator rule root-spec-required-when-metadata-exists should fail packet roots that carry description.json or graph-metadata.json without spec.md
Validator rule graph-source-docs-required-for-active-packet should fail packet roots whose graph metadata has an empty source_docs array
Validator rule save-lineage-required-on-fresh-graph-save should fail fresh graph metadata writes that omit save_lineage after the wrapper fix cutoff
Validator rule packet-identity-normalization should warn or fail when description, continuity, and graph packet identities do not normalize to the same packet-relative path
Validator rule description-newer-than-graph-on-recent-packet should flag recent packets whose description freshness materially exceeds graph freshness

focus: Inspect the built/runtime save_lineage path, classify 007/008/009/010 packet-root disposition, and convert the validator draft into concrete rollout-ready rules with grandfathering.

graphEvents.label: Built workflow.js calls refreshGraphMetadata(validatedSpecFolderPath) with no options, so same_pass never reaches the runtime graph refresh path
Built parser/schema in mcp_server/dist omit save_lineage entirely, proving the shipped runtime bundle is out of sync with source
007, 008, 009, and 010 are all active coordination-parent packet roots with description.json and graph-metadata.json but no root canonical docs and no git history for spec.md

Roll out CANONICAL_SAVE_ROOT_SPEC_REQUIRED and CANONICAL_SAVE_SOURCE_DOCS_REQUIRED after targeted packet repair, keep identity/freshness warning-only first, and gate CANONICAL_SAVE_LINEAGE_REQUIRED behind a post-fix cutoff and backfill

focus: Cross-validate the two P0 findings across the full 026 tree, verify whether the save_lineage fix needs wrapper plus dist repair rather than a caller-only patch, inspect other metadata fields for similar writeback gaps, and pre-stage the research.md synthesis structure.

graphEvents.label: Full 026 rescan confirms 007 through 010 remain the only active packet roots outside research/review with metadata files but no root spec.md
Twenty-one nested research iteration directories carry description.json and graph-metadata.json without spec.md, establishing an expected artifact exemption rather than a new packet-root defect
mcp_server/api/indexing.ts still exposes refreshGraphMetadata(specFolder) and drops GraphMetadataRefreshOptions before the parser can persist saveLineage
Across 026, keywords and memoryNameHistory are universal, importance_tier and key_topics miss only one archived review file, but save_lineage is missing from every graph-metadata payload
Workflow caller, public indexing wrapper, parser implementation, and built dist artifacts must preserve GraphMetadataRefreshOptions end-to-end
Extend workflow canonical-save and graph refresh tests to assert save_lineage same_pass plus indexing-wrapper option passthrough

focus: Finalize the metadata writeback audit, convert the validator draft into rollout-ready packet-root rules, and choose the least-destructive remediation pattern for 007/008/009/010.

graphEvents.label: description.json memorySequence, memoryNameHistory, and keywords are intentionally stable and already covered by generation/save tracking tests rather than exhibiting writeback gaps
graph-metadata derived trigger_phrases, key_topics, importance_tier, status, and causal_summary still write on coordination parents even without root spec docs; entities can legitimately remain empty
save_lineage remains the only systemic writeback gap because the indexing wrapper still drops refresh options before the parser/runtime path can persist them

Finalize five packet-root validator candidates scoped to root-spec presence, source-doc provenance, save-lineage persistence, packet identity normalization, and description-vs-graph freshness without duplicating existing continuity/fingerprint checks
Repair 007/008/009/010 as coordination-parent roots with minimal summary docs instead of stubs or archive moves

focus: Synthesis draft for canonical save invariants research.md and iteration-008 consolidation

graphEvents.label: Drafted research.md with executive summary, invariant catalogue, divergences, P0s, H-56-1 scope, validator assertions, remediation plan, and open questions.

focus: Polish research.md to near-final quality by tightening the executive summary, hardening the validator assertion table, and clarifying the sibling implementation-child remediation waves.

graphEvents.label: Executive summary tightened into a four-paragraph handoff covering scope, P0 defects, validator rollout, and wave plan.
Validator assertion table now uses explicit trigger logic, uppercase severities, explicit grandfathering, migration paths, and non-overlap notes.
Implementation handoff narrowed to sibling child 002-canonical-save-hardening with Wave A/B/C dependencies, day estimates, and success criteria.
